title: The Gang Wins the Big Game

jokes.text: The word is "superstition," not "stupidstition." / I know what the word is, Charlie. I'm saying your Green Man suit is a stupid superstition.
jokes.setup: Mac apparently mispronounces 'superstition' as 'stupidstition'
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals he was intentionally combining 'stupid' and 'superstition' to mock Charlie
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that makes the audience initially think Mac is dumb, then reveals he's being deliberately insulting

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The word is "superstition," not "stupidstition."

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I know what the word is, Charlie. I'm saying your Green Man suit is a stupid superstition.

jokes.text: I'm not a Cowboy fan, I'm a Tony Romo fan, and he retired, so now I bleed green.
jokes.setup: Mac being accused of being a Cowboys fan
jokes.punchline: His loyalty is to one specific player, not a team, and he switches allegiances when the player retires
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's shallow, bandwagon mentality in a specific way

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm not a Cowboy fan, I'm a Tony Romo fan, and he retired, so now I bleed green.

jokes.text: What's going on with your eye? / Nothing, I got a... I think I got an eyelash in it. You're, like, scratching it and it's blinking... I just, I slept on it funny.
jokes.setup: Dee clearly has a serious eye problem
jokes.punchline: Her obvious denial with increasingly weak excuses
jokes.explanation: Sets up ongoing visual gag of Dee's deteriorating eye condition with typical denial

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's going on with your eye?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Nothing, I got a... I think I got an eyelash in it. You're, like, scratching it and it's blinking... I just, I slept on it funny.

jokes.text: Chug, chug, chug... That's 15. Post that, Jack, post it, baby.
jokes.setup: Fans trying to impress with drinking
jokes.punchline: The pathetic social media documentation of excessive drinking
jokes.explanation: Generic commentary on social media culture, not particularly sharp

dialogue.line: Chug, chug, chug... That's 15. Post that, Jack, post it, baby.

jokes.text: No big, giant, ridiculous hands. / What? What? D-Don't make me go without them.
jokes.setup: Someone has comically oversized foam hands
jokes.punchline: Their desperate attachment to these ridiculous accessories
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag, relies on costume more than wit

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: No big, giant, ridiculous hands.

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: What? What? D-Don't make me go without them.

jokes.text: You have literally picked out the biggest pieces of shit in the city. / I got to agree with that. These guys are pieces of shit.
jokes.setup: The gang criticizing Frank's guest choices
jokes.punchline: Complete lack of self-awareness that they're equally terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their complete lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You have literally picked out the biggest pieces of shit in the city.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I got to agree with that. These guys are pieces of shit.

jokes.text: What is in your mouth? / Chew. / Awesome, cool, good to see you.
jokes.setup: Dee asks what someone is chewing
jokes.punchline: Just 'chew' - implying chewing tobacco, and Dee's disgusted but polite response

dialogue.line: What is in your mouth?

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: Chew.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Awesome, cool, good to see you.

jokes.text: Your nose is bleeding as we speak. / Okay, I came up portside on a horse, and he was a little quicker than me... that's lesson learned.
jokes.setup: Cricket's nose is bleeding and he's being kicked out
jokes.punchline: His casual explanation involves a horse race mishap with nautical terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Cricket moment mixing horse racing with naval terms, very character-specific

dialogue.line: Your nose is bleeding as we speak.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Okay, I came up portside on a horse, and he was a little quicker than me... that's lesson learned.

jokes.text: Rex represents the best that Philadelphia has to offer. / Either way, Cricks, you're out.
jokes.setup: Dee praising Rex as the best Philadelphia has to offer
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by dismissing Cricket, showing arbitrary standards
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's arbitrary judgment and hypocrisy

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Rex represents the best that Philadelphia has to offer.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Either way, Cricks, you're out.

jokes.text: Is this a bar? / Nice. Nice. / Fully stocked, baby.

jokes.explanation: Basic physical gag, not particularly clever

dialogue.line: Is this a bar?
Nice. Nice.
Fully stocked, baby.

jokes.text: L. E. S. Eagles.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to spell Eagles
jokes.punchline: He spells it completely wrong but with confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - confidently wrong in a very specific way

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: L. E. S. Eagles.

jokes.text: This is, like, the tenth time. / Yeah, i-it turns out there's not that many songs about Philadelphia.
jokes.setup: Complaint about singing the same song repeatedly

jokes.explanation: Good observational humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: This is, like, the tenth time.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, i-it turns out there's not that many songs about Philadelphia.

jokes.text: All right, fine, it's me. Cricket. / Cricket. / Yes, it's me, it's me. I snuck on, all right? This damn nose.
jokes.setup: Someone's nose is bleeding
jokes.punchline: Cricket revealing he disguised himself and snuck on the bus
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Cricket's desperation and resourcefulness

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: All right, fine, it's me. Cricket.
Cricket.

dialogue.speaker: Cricket
dialogue.line: Yes, it's me, it's me. I snuck on, all right? This damn nose.

jokes.text: I don't want to set a precedent of little mishaps setting us back, so... Cricket, you're back in.
jokes.setup: Mac talking about not letting mishaps set them back
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by letting Cricket back in because Charlie's gone
jokes.explanation: Good character inconsistency showing Mac's poor leadership

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't want to set a precedent of little mishaps setting us back, so... Cricket, you're back in.

jokes.text: Sh-She don't even like football. / Yeah, football sucks. But if Charlie missed the bus, he missed the bus. I'm not missing a second of my dreamboat, Tommy Terrific.
jokes.setup: Waitress doesn't like football
jokes.punchline: She's obsessed with Tom Brady despite hating the sport
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - hates football but loves Brady

dialogue.line: Sh-She don't even like football.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Yeah, football sucks. But if Charlie missed the bus, he missed the bus. I'm not missing a second of my dreamboat, Tommy Terrific.

jokes.text: Go, Pats. Go, Pats. Go, Pats. / Whoa, whoa, whoa. Drive. Drive. Go, Pats. Go, Pats. Shit, pothole.
jokes.setup: Waitress cheering for Patriots while on a bus full of Eagles fans

dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa.

dialogue.line: Drive. Drive. Go, Pats. Go, Pats.

dialogue.line: Shit, pothole.

jokes.text: You're involved in that pyramid scheme, too? / No. No hocking berries at the Super Bowl, Rex. Just be meat, okay? / Well... take off your shirt, please.
jokes.setup: Rex is involved in a berry pyramid scheme
jokes.punchline: Mac tells him to 'be meat' and take off his shirt, presumably for visual appeal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Mac's weird objectification

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're involved in that pyramid scheme, too? / No. No hocking berries at the Super Bowl, Rex. Just be meat, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Rex
dialogue.line: Well... take off your shirt, please.

jokes.text: No, no, no. They don't have axles there. But I can get a new pair of jean shorts.
jokes.setup: Frank suggesting going to Walmart for bus parts
jokes.punchline: Admits they don't have what's needed but focuses on personal shopping instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - completely self-centered priorities during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No, no, no. They don't have axles there. But I can get a new pair of jean shorts.

jokes.text: We invite the wrong crowd, and now we've got people going on expeditions for jean shorts. And hocking magical berries and rooting for the Patriots?
jokes.setup: Dee listing everything going wrong
jokes.punchline: The absurd catalog of problems including 'expeditions for jean shorts'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with absurd specificity

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We invite the wrong crowd, and now we've got people going on expeditions for jean shorts. And hocking magical berries and rooting for the Patriots?

jokes.text: Well... E-A-G-L-E-S. Eagles.
jokes.setup: Dee's dramatic speech about never making it to the Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely inappropriate cheerleading response
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character response - Charlie completely missing the mood

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well... E-A-G-L-E-S. Eagles.

jokes.text: Well, we made it to the Super Bowl. That was easy. / Yeah, it was incredibly smooth.
jokes.setup: The previous scene of everything going wrong
jokes.punchline: Cut to them already at the Super Bowl acting like nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Excellent scene transition joke with perfect ironic timing

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, we made it to the Super Bowl. That was easy.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was incredibly smooth.

jokes.text: Let's-let's cool it on the boos, you know? That's-that's kind of, like, what we're famous for, you know? We-we boo ourselves, we boo the Eagles, we boo Santa Claus.

jokes.punchline: Mac lists Philadelphia's reputation for booing everything, including Santa
jokes.explanation: Good reference to Philadelphia's notorious fan behavior, escalating list ending with Santa Claus

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Let's-let's cool it on the boos, you know? That's-that's kind of, like, what we're famous for, you know? We-we boo ourselves, we boo the Eagles, we boo Santa Claus.

jokes.text: We-we boo ourselves, we boo the Eagles, we boo Santa Claus.

jokes.explanation: Great escalating list referencing real Philadelphia sports culture

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We-we boo ourselves, we boo the Eagles, we boo Santa Claus.

jokes.text: Okay, "Everybody look at me, I'm a stupid Pats fan, making up stuff about Dee's eye."

dialogue.line: Okay, "Everybody look at me, I'm a stupid Pats fan, making up stuff about Dee's eye."

jokes.text: How did I move all the way to Minnesota, and I'm still waiting on you people? / I-I don't know this guy. / No, you never waited on us. We don't live in Minnesota, bud.
jokes.setup: Waiter complaining about moving to Minnesota but still dealing with them

dialogue.speaker: Gang
dialogue.line: I-I don't know this guy.

dialogue.line: No, you never waited on us. We don't live in Minnesota, bud.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I tied his shoelaces together.

jokes.text: This Minnesota rube, he's not ready for your big-city shenanigans, Frank.

jokes.text: Ding-dong here's getting honored with the rest of the vets. I'm gonna sneak down there with him. When we get close to the field, I'm gonna let you in on a little plan I've whipped up.
jokes.setup: Frank talking about a veteran being honored

jokes.text: So, you know what, you can go, but I'm, I'm just gonna cover you up so people don't know that you're from Philadelphia.

dialogue.line: So, you know what, you can go, but I'm, I'm just gonna cover you up so people don't know that you're from Philadelphia.

jokes.text: Luxury suite? Napkins? Utensils? We're going soft up here in this ivory tower.

dialogue.line: Are you pissing?

dialogue.line: Piss, Frank, piss.

dialogue.line: Just keep peeing. I don't know. It's not updating.

jokes.text: We are Philadelphia. These guys are a total embarrassment. We blew it, man.
jokes.setup: Mac watching the fans embarrass themselves
jokes.punchline: Complete lack of self-awareness that he's part of the problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's hypocrisy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We are Philadelphia. These guys are a total embarrassment. We blew it, man.

jokes.text: Because that's who he is, man. And you know who we are? We're losers. All of Philadelphia. We're angry, and we're mean and we're cruel.
jokes.setup: Mac despairs about Brady having the ball
jokes.punchline: Mac's brutal honesty about Philadelphia's character and their own nature
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of genuine self-reflection from Mac, acknowledging their toxic nature

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because that's who he is, man. And you know who we are? We're losers. All of Philadelphia. We're angry, and we're mean and we're cruel.

jokes.text: No. Those guys, those guys are Philadelphia. They bust their ass every day just to get through.
jokes.setup: Mac calls the group embarrassing
jokes.punchline: Frank defends them as representing real Philadelphia working class
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly thoughtful moment from Frank showing understanding of working class dignity

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No. Those guys, those guys are Philadelphia. They bust their ass every day just to get through.

jokes.text: Those guys, those guys are Philadelphia. They bust their ass every day just to get through. And then, on a Sunday, they put all their hopes into the Eagles.
jokes.setup: Mac criticizing the fans
jokes.punchline: Frank's surprisingly insightful defense of working-class fans
jokes.explanation: Surprising character depth from Frank with genuine emotional insight

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Those guys, those guys are Philadelphia. They bust their ass every day just to get through. And then, on a Sunday, they put all their hopes into the Eagles.

jokes.text: And they get angry. And that anger builds into a stone of fury. And if it could just be released, then we could feel something different.
jokes.setup: Frank's speech about Philadelphia fans
jokes.punchline: Connecting their emotional pain to his literal kidney stone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor connecting Frank's physical pain to the city's emotional pain - genuinely moving and funny

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: And they get angry. And that anger builds into a stone of fury. And if it could just be released, then we could feel something different.

jokes.text: And that anger builds into a stone of fury. And if it could just be released, then we could feel something different.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining Philadelphia fan psychology

jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor connecting kidney stone to Philadelphia's emotional state, one of the best jokes in the episode

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: And that anger builds into a stone of fury. And if it could just be released, then we could feel something different.

jokes.text: Do it for the Birds, Frank. Do it for the whole city of Philadelphia, man, push. / Brady, with White to his right, takes the snap. / Do it like Rocky, man. Push.

dialogue.line: Do it like Rocky, man. Push.

jokes.explanation: Perfect Philadelphia reference applied to absurd medical situation, great economy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do it like Rocky, man. Push.

dialogue.line: You did it.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm dry.

jokes.text: Yeah, you're gonna have to get back in that trap. / Aw, come on, man. You just told me to get out of it. / Yeah, that was before Brady had the ball.
jokes.setup: Charlie being told to get back in some uncomfortable position
jokes.punchline: Mac's arbitrary rules based on game situation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist sports superstition with Mac's changing demands

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, you're gonna have to get back in that trap.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Aw, come on, man. You just told me to get out of it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, that was before Brady had the ball.

jokes.text: You just told me to get out of it. / Yeah, that was before Brady had the ball. / Does it have to be the same leg or... / Same leg.
jokes.setup: Charlie must get back in whatever superstitious position
jokes.punchline: Charlie asks about specific leg requirements for the superstition
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of superstition specificity, Charlie's practical question about impossible requirements

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You just told me to get out of it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, that was before Brady had the ball.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Does it have to be the same leg or...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Same leg.

jokes.text: He's hit. He stumbles. / Do it, do it now. / He's throwing. He is going deep for the end zone, and it is... batted around... and incomplete. And the game is over.
jokes.setup: Final play tension with superstition
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing of Charlie's action with game-ending incompletion
jokes.explanation: Excellent climactic payoff bringing together all superstition threads

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: He's hit. He stumbles.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do it, do it now.

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: He's throwing. He is going deep for the end zone, and it is... batted around... and incomplete. And the game is over.

jokes.text: We did it. The superstitions are real. / Charlie, you did it. You did it, you were right. The superstitions were real. You did it, man.
jokes.setup: Eagles win the game
jokes.punchline: Complete belief that their ridiculous superstitions caused the victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their delusion and Charlie vindication

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We did it. The superstitions are real.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie, you did it. You did it, you were right. The superstitions were real. You did it, man.
